docs.text: .image1{ border-radius: 100%; overflow: hidden; width: 135px; } .image2{ width:210px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:20%; } .image3{ width:95px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:5%; } .image4{ width:140px; overflow: hidden; border-radius:5%; } Guanxuan (Grayson) Jiang Feel free to reach out to me via gjiang240@connect.hkust-gz.edu.cn My name is Guanxuan Jiang (蒋冠宣), and I am an MPhil (Master of Philosophy) student in the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou) with a full scholarship. My research interests are in the fields of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Human Behavior, Artificial Intelligence (AI), Virtual Reality (VR) and Augmented Reality (AR). I am member of Center for M etaverse and C omputational C reativity (MC 2 ) Lab . I obtained my Bachelor of Science with Honour from the University of Sheffield in the department of Computer Science with one-year Study Abroad at the Chinese University of Hong Kong , supported by the Global Opportunity Organization and the Turning Scheme. Education September 2023 - June 2025 (expected) - Master of Philosophy (MPhil) Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST), Guangzhou, China Computational Media and Arts Thrust (CMA) , Information Hub College of Future Technology (CFT) Center for M etaverse and C omputational C reativity (MC 2 ) Lab Keywords: Human-Computer Interaction (HCI), Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Metaverse Supervisor: Prof Pan Hui , FRENG , FIEEE , MAE , Distinguished Scientist of ACM Co-supervisors: Prof Fugee Tsung and Prof Yuyang Wang September 2020 - June 2023 - Bachelor of Science with Honour (BSc.) University of Sheffield (UoS), Sheffield, England Department of Computer Science , Faculty of Engineering Major: Computer Science Dissertation: ARMessageBoard: Location-Based Social Interaction with Augmented Reality (Distinction) Dissertation Advisors: Prof Nafise Sadat Moosavi and Prof Yue Li September 2021 - June 2022 - Study Abroad Chinses University of Hong Kong (CUHK), Shatin, Hong Kong Shaw College , Department of Computer Science and Engineering July 2021 - August 2021 - Summer School Peking University , Beijing, China Working Experience May 2022 - October 2022 - Research Assistant Xi'an Jiaotong-Liverpool University , Suzhou, China Department of Computing, School of Advanced Technology Collaborating Supervisor: Prof Yue Li Keywords: Human Computer Interaction (HCI), Virtual Reality (VR), Augmented Reality (AR) March 2021 - June 2021 - Science and Technology Innovation Division Beijing ANTIY Network Security Technology Co. LTD , Beijing, China Keywords: Attack Capture System, ANTIY(RWD) Fundings June 2022 - Summer Undergraduate Research Fellowships ( SURF ) The Support of the Research Fund from the Department of Computing, School of Advanced Technology, XJTLU June 2021 - Study Abroad with Finance Support 85% Tuition Waiver from the Global Opportunity Organization , University of Sheffield, £20,000 November 2021 - Turning Scheme Grant from United King Government The Turning Scheme is Held by United Kingdom Government for Abroad (National), £3,000 Awards September 2023 - June 2025(expected) - Full Postgraduate Studentship (PGS) Award from Guangzhou Government The PGS Rate is CNY10,000 Per Month November 2022 - Second Prize in the Final of the 5th China Competition on Virtual Reality The Project Named ‘Virtual Chemistry Laboratory‘, Among 1,000+ Teams, CCVR2022 (National Award) Januray 2021 - Professional Behaviors Team Award in Global Engineering Challenge Projects United Kingdom Standard for Professional Engineering Competence (UKSPEC) , University of Sheffield June 2020 - International Computer Science Excellence Scholarship Excellent Academic Performance before First-year Entrance, The University of Sheffield, £2,000 Extracurricular Activities July 2023 - 2023 Red Bird Challenge Camp - Nansha, Guangzhou, Guangdong, China May 2022 - 2022 Conference on Human Factors in Computing System - New Orleans, United States Attending Lectures: C12: Inclusion Techniques for Team Practices in HCI & C13: Wearable Technology Design and Accessibility Considerations Course. July 2020 - TENCENT Rhinoceros Bird Elite Research Camp - Shenzhen, Guangdong, China Teachings September 2025 - Teaching Assistant - HKUST-GZ, Guangzhou, China UCMP6030 Cross-disciplinary Design Thinking Service & Volunteer June 2021 - June 2022 - Chinese Students and Scholars Association, Sheffield (CSSA-Sheffield) - Sheffield, England Academic Department July 2019 - YIK International Volunteer Service Center - Maldives, Himmafushi Volunteered in Himmafushi, one of the Inhabited Islands of Kaafu Atoll in the Maldive, over 120 hours. docs.text: Blog📓 Welcome to my blog space. I'm excited to share my life experiences and thoughts with all of you here. On this platform, I'll be posting updates regularly, offering interesting stories, insights, and my perspectives on various topics. I hope these posts resonate with you or provide some inspiration. I look forward to your subscriptions and interactions. Have a wonderful day, and thank you for your support! Big Day 🎉 I will document some of the big moments in my life here. I won't forget these times and the unforgettable memories they bring. University of Sheffield 🎓 Taken at the University of Sheffield, Sheffield, England on 17 July 2023. This is my graduation ceremony at the University of Sheffield. I'm thrilled to have completed my undergraduate studies here; it's a place filled with memories. I want to express my gratitude for everything that happened here—the friends I've made, the restaurants I've visited, and everything else. Anyway, thank you! Please refer to the video I uploaded on YouTube . Sketch🤔 🚧Coming soon... Trip ✈️ 🚧Coming soon... Technical Log 💻 🚧Coming soon... Page last revised on: 2024-08-21

docs.text: Taken at the University of Sheffield, Sheffield, England on 17 July 2023. This is my graduation ceremony at the University of Sheffield. I'm thrilled to have completed my undergraduate studies here; it's a place filled with memories. I want to express my gratitude for everything that happened here—the friends I've made, the restaurants I've visited, and everything else. Anyway, thank you! Please refer to the video I uploaded on YouTube .

docs.title: Technical Log 💻

docs.text: Selected Projects My research interests are board, encompassing, but not limited to, human-computer interaction, human-AI interaction, virtual reality, and augmented reality. Here, I will showcase some of my past work demos. If you find any of it insightful, please feel free to discuss and contact me. Virtual Reality ChemistryVR: Virtual Reality Chemistry Experiment Simulator These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2022. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Experiments play a crucial role in chemistry education, yet they often require extensive equipment, ample space, and pose potential safety hazards. Virtual Reality (VR) offers an innovative solution to these challenges through immersive and interactive simulations. In response, we developed a virtual laboratory aligned with the ninth-grade chemistry curriculum from the People's Education Press in China. This system encompasses safety training and detailed tutorials, allowing students to engage with interactive simulations and observe realistic experimental phenomena in a secure environment. By practicing chemistry experiments in VR, students can avoid dangerous and unforeseen outcomes resulting from experimental mistakes. Our system provides a risk-free method that effectively facilitates experiential and practice-oriented learning in chemistry. Furthermore, it seamlessly integrates theoretical knowledge with practical applications, closely following the official textbook. This comprehensive approach enables students to transition smoothly from theory to practice, deepening their understanding of chemistry concepts. As a result, students are better equipped for both standardized tests and real-world laboratory situations. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube . Augmented Reality Blending Social Interaction Realms: Harmonizing Online and Offline Interactions through Augmented Reality These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2023. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Online social media has redefined human interaction, yet often at the expense of local engagement. To bridge this gap, we explored the innovative use of Augmented Reality (AR) to enhance location-specific interactions. We developed ARMessageBoard, a pioneering prototype that integrates AR with the user's surroundings to create virtual message boards. This approach aims to enrich the local context often overlooked by digital platforms. In our study with 15 participants, we demonstrated that combining AR with social media could better integrate virtual and real-world interactions, offering a new dimension to socio-digital exchanges. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube . Human-AI Collabration 🚧Coming soon... Human Behaviour 🚧Coming soon... Human Computer Interaction 🚧Coming soon... Page last revised on: 2024-08-21

docs.text: These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2022. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Experiments play a crucial role in chemistry education, yet they often require extensive equipment, ample space, and pose potential safety hazards. Virtual Reality (VR) offers an innovative solution to these challenges through immersive and interactive simulations. In response, we developed a virtual laboratory aligned with the ninth-grade chemistry curriculum from the People's Education Press in China. This system encompasses safety training and detailed tutorials, allowing students to engage with interactive simulations and observe realistic experimental phenomena in a secure environment. By practicing chemistry experiments in VR, students can avoid dangerous and unforeseen outcomes resulting from experimental mistakes. Our system provides a risk-free method that effectively facilitates experiential and practice-oriented learning in chemistry. Furthermore, it seamlessly integrates theoretical knowledge with practical applications, closely following the official textbook. This comprehensive approach enables students to transition smoothly from theory to practice, deepening their understanding of chemistry concepts. As a result, students are better equipped for both standardized tests and real-world laboratory situations. Please refer to the demo video I uploaded on YouTube .

docs.text: These images are screenshots and photographs from this project, 2023. @keyframes scroll { 0% { transform: translateX(0%); } 100% { transform: translateX(-50%); } } Online social media has redefined human interaction, yet often at the expense of local engagement. To bridge this gap, we explored the innovative use of Augmented Reality (AR) to enhance location-specific interactions. We developed ARMessageBoard, a pioneering prototype that integrates AR with the user's surroundings to create virtual message boards. This approach aims to enrich the local context often overlooked by digital platforms. In our study with 15 participants, we demonstrated that combining AR with social media could better integrate virtual and real-world interactions, offering a new dimension to socio-digital exchanges.
